per_module.note: seed 42 collapsed (recall yes=0.389); excl. collapse mean=0.976

analysis.pathway_works: With coordinates removed from the text, shared gating reaches 0.983 +/- 0.005 balanced accuracy. Location reaches the model ONLY through the coord channel, so this is clean evidence the spatial pathway conveys usable information to the LLM.
analysis.per_module_verdict: Per-module gating gives no accuracy benefit over shared and is far less stable: 1 of 3 seeds collapsed to 0.68. Prefer the shared gate. The interpretable per-module gate read-out also did not materialize (gates stayed ~0).
analysis.gate_caveat: Gates stayed ~0.002 in ALL runs (leaky AND no-leak), yet in the no-leak runs that tiny gate is load-bearing (the text is identical across examples, so the gate is the only path to the answer). Gate MAGNITUDE is therefore NOT a usage readout in this architecture. This corrects the earlier per_module_gating.json reading of '~0 gates => pathway unused'.
analysis.task_caveat: coord_input_dim=3 feeds elevation directly as the 3rd coordinate, so 'is elevation>122?' reduces to thresholding one input number — the channel passes information, but this is not spatial REASONING (inferring elevation from location).
analysis.next: coord_2d_noleak: lat/lon only (no elevation input), no text coords. Forces the model to LEARN the lat/lon->elevation map. coord_2d_noleak vs coord_3d_noleak is the proper leak-free redo of Step 1; the gap = how much the model relies on elevation-as-input vs learned geography. See configs/coord_2d_noleak.yaml.
